metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a wrap-up/verification child (or explicit epic-close checklist) that audits the .joe-janitor/report-2026-07-02.md Critical/High list against the closed children roster and confirms all signed verdicts.
findings.finding: Parent AC5 ('All children closed with signed completion verdicts; report's Critical/High set addressed') is a meta-closure criterion with no child providing a mechanism to audit or certify it. No child is responsible for verifying that the FULL Critical/High set from the audit report has been addressed, nor that each child has been closed with a signed verdict.
findings.location: Parent AC5 — no child covers this
findings.suggested_fix: Create a final child 'Epic closure audit: verify all Critical/High findings addressed and all children carry signed verdicts' that explicitly maps report findings to children before the epic is closed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback/decision rule to the epic plan for blocked plan-review at claim (e.g. 'revise and re-review before proceeding') and for completion-gate unavailability (e.g. '--force-close with documented justification'), so an LLM executor has an unambiguous protocol for both failure branches.
findings.finding: The plan's Success Criteria and AC mandate that each remediation child lands through 'Gerrit (LLM-Review + Verified), with plan-review at claim and a signed completion verdict at close' — but no fallback/decision rule is specified for the case where plan-review BLOCKs the child's claim or where the child's own completion gate fails to produce a certifiable verdict. An LLM executor driving the epic has no protocol for these failure branches.
findings.location: Success Criteria / AC — 'Each approved remediation lands as its own child through Gerrit (LLM-Review + Verified), with a rebar-ticket: trailer, plan-review at claim, and a signed completion verdict at close.'
findings.suggested_fix: Under the Success Criteria or a new 'Process rules' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC item 4's effort-framed deferral with an enumerated list of the specific approved remediation findings (by risk number or finding ID from the audit report) that must have children created, so scope is self-contained and the criterion is verifiable.
findings.finding: AC item 4 ('The remaining approved remediations are decomposed into children, each with AC + file-impact') is effort-framed and defers real scope decisions to the executor: it does not state what the remaining remediations are, only that they will be decomposed. An executing agent cannot determine the set of in-scope findings without consulting the external audit report, which is referenced but not summarised in the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Inline the set of approved remediations from the audit report (e.g., 'Risks 3–7 from .joe-janitor/report-2026-07-02.md: [Risk 3: X, Risk 4: Y, ...]') and reframe AC 4 as a durable end-state: 'Children exist for Risks 3–N, each with AC and file-impact listed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite AC item 3 as a durable, observable outcome — e.g., 'A closed child ticket exists linking commit(s) for the stale-reference fixes, with a signed completion verdict' — so it is verifiable by inspection rather than being a process step.
findings.finding: AC item 3 ('Stale-reference doc/comment fixes (already landed) tracked under a child') describes a one-time process transition (creating/linking a ticket) rather than a durable, observable end-state, and 'tracked under a child' is effort-framed rather than outcome-framed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a Success Criterion covering stale-reference doc/comment fixes (e.g. 'Already-landed stale-reference fixes are tracked under a closed child with a signed completion verdict') so AC #3 maps to a defined done-state.
findings.finding: AC #3 ('Stale-reference doc/comment fixes (already landed) tracked under a child') has no corresponding Success Criterion — the Success Criteria section is silent on this deliverable, making AC #3 an orphan criterion that measures work undescribed in Success Criteria.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria line 3 vs. Success Criteria section
findings.suggested_fix: In the Success Criteria section, add a bullet: 'Stale-reference doc/comment fixes (already landed prior to this epic) are accounted for under a dedicated child, closed with a signed completion verdict and a `rebar-ticket:` trailer.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan (or expand-contract sequence) for the `has_llm_steps` gate fix — e.g., a documented rollback commit/tag, a short grace-period flag, or a pre-fix audit of all `ALLOW_LLM` usages confirming none are legitimate before the gate hardens.
findings.finding: The `has_llm_steps`/`ALLOW_LLM` gate-bypass fix is a behavioral breaking change (workflows currently passing the gate will be blocked) with no rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract sequence specified.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'The Critical `ALLOW_LLM` gate bypass is fixed'; AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Before landing the gate fix, run a codebase-wide grep for `ALLOW_LLM` usage and document findings in the child's AC. If any legitimate uses exist, sequence as: (1) migrate those uses, (2) harden gate, (3) tag a rollback ref. Document the rollback ref in the child's completion verdict.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and update the authoritative doc/ADR for the `has_llm_steps` gate (or create one if absent) as part of the Risk 1 child, describing the corrected detection behavior and what `ALLOW_LLM` now means.
findings.finding: The corrected `has_llm_steps` gate contract (what the gate now detects and blocks) has no corresponding doc or ADR update planned, leaving the authoritative behavioral spec stale after the fix lands.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'both shipped gate workflows are detected by `has_llm_steps`'; AC line 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale in the Risk 1 AC or Success Criteria stating WHY the fix-the-detector approach (extending `_scan` to handle bare step-array arms) is preferred over alternatives, and confirm whether the fix is already landed (so the child's scope is Gerrit tracking + sign-off, not fresh implementation).
findings.finding: The Success Criteria and AC for Risk 1 describe the `has_llm_steps` fix and regression test as future work ('is fixed', 'are detected by has_llm_steps'), but the fix and the regression test already exist in the codebase (`runs.py:286-317`, `test_agent_op_safeguard.py:97-129`). The plan gives no positive rationale for the fix approach chosen — it is borrowed implicitly from the audit report's recommendation — so if the child plan deviates (e.g., by choosing an alternative mechanism like a workflow-header flag), there is no stated constraint anchoring it to the fix-the-detector approach.
findings.location: Success Criteria — Risk 1 / AC line: 'Risk 1 (has_llm_steps ALLOW_LLM bypass) remediated + regression test'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Success Criteria: 'The fix extends `has_llm_steps._scan` to handle bare step-array arms (v3 `then`/`else`/`body`) rather than schema-level annotation, because the detector is the single call-site for the gate and already covers the v2 dict-shape — keeping blast radius minimal and the fix self-contained. If the implementation is already on a branch, the child scope is Gerrit landing + signed close, not a new implementation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Establish explicit landing order: ce94 must be merged (or co-authored) before 16df; 16df's AC must verify against the post-ce94 gate logic.
findings.finding: ce94 (fix ALLOW_LLM bypass at mcp_server.py:946, inside build_server) and 16df (split the entire 703-LOC build_server at mcp_server.py:295-997) have an unacknowledged ordering dependency: the Critical gate-bypass fix targets a line that lives physically inside the function being restructured by 16df. No coordination or ordering is stated between these two children.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency note in 16df referencing ce94; verify 16df's 'gating unchanged' coverage test runs the has_llm_steps regression from ce94.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that b043's near-cap list explicitly defers transition_compute to 3777 (remove it from b043's scope or add a cross-reference).
findings.finding: b043 (size split) explicitly lists 'transition transition_compute' as a near-cap target to split, directly overlapping with 3777 which is the dedicated child for decomposing transition_compute. Dual ownership of the same refactor with no stated coordination.
findings.suggested_fix: Strike 'transition transition_compute' from b043's near-cap list and add a note that this is handled by 3777.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Establish ordering (4419 before 3777, or 3777 must include verdict_hash removal) and document it in both children.
findings.finding: 3777 (decompose transition_compute, extracting _close_ticket) and 4419 (remove the dead verdict_hash parameter from transition.py/txn.py) both operate on transition_compute with no stated ordering. If 3777 lands first, the extracted _close_ticket carries the dead verdict_hash parameter that 4419 must then chase into the new module; if 4419 lands first, 3777 extracts already-clean code but neither child acknowledges this dependency.
findings.suggested_fix: Land 4419 before 3777, or explicitly add verdict_hash removal to 3777's scope and note the dependency on 4419 being superseded.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit ordering: 9d7a before 16df; update 16df's AC to verify the config-aware readonly helper is preserved.
findings.finding: 9d7a (fix readonly gate in runner.py:566-571 and mcp_server.py:208-223 to use a single config-aware helper) and 16df (split build_server covering mcp_server.py:295-997 including the tool registration that uses the readonly gate) have overlapping scope in mcp_server.py with no stated ordering. 16df's AC requires 'gating unchanged' but the gate it is meant to preserve is the broken one until 9d7a lands.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a cross-reference in 16df requiring 9d7a to have landed; add a specific AC line in 16df verifying the config-aware readonly path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State landing order between ebd8 and 92d2 for gate_ops.py, or co-author the gate_ops.py import changes.
findings.finding: ebd8 (reroute gate_ops.py to use from rebar import _reads, changing its import structure) and 92d2 (update gate_ops.py:50,117 to import public names from completion.py) both modify gate_ops.py's import block concurrently with no stated ordering or cross-reference.
findings.suggested_fix: Land 92d2 (public name promotion) before ebd8 (import reroute), since ebd8 needs the public names to exist before switching the import form.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
